text: Continuous Integration is a development practice where developers frequently merge their code changes into a shared repository. Each merge triggers an *automated build*, which compiles the code and packages it automatically. This is followed by *running tests* against the new version to verify that everything works correctly. The main goal of CI is *catching issues early* before they become bigger problems in later stages. All of this happens through *CI pipelines*, which are sequences of automated steps defined by the team. These steps ensure that code changes meet quality standards. By using CI, teams release more reliable software, reduce debugging time, and deliver updates faster.
